result.stdout:
Description:
  kli — interactive KQL shell for Azure Data Explorer

Usage:
  kli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config  Manage kli configuration

commands.payload:
Description:
  kli — interactive KQL shell for Azure Data Explorer

Usage:
  kli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  config  Manage kli configuration

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage kli configuration

Usage:
  kli config [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add-cluster <name> <url>  Add or update a named cluster profile
  use <name>                Switch the active cluster profile
  list-clusters             List all configured cluster profiles
  remove-cluster <name>     Remove a named cluster profile
  set-cluster <url>         Update the active profile's cluster URL
  set-database <database>   Update the active profile's default database
  show                      Show current configuration

commands.payload:
Description:
  Manage kli configuration

Usage:
  kli config [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add-cluster <name> <url>  Add or update a named cluster profile
  use <name>                Switch the active cluster profile
  list-clusters             List all configured cluster profiles
  remove-cluster <name>     Remove a named cluster profile
  set-cluster <url>         Update the active profile's cluster URL
  set-database <database>   Update the active profile's default database
  show                      Show current configuration

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add or update a named cluster profile

Usage:
  kli config add-cluster <name> <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <name>  Profile name (e.g. prod, dev)
  <url>   Kusto cluster URL

Options:
  --database <database>  Default database for this profile
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
